title: sambucus racemosa f. nuda
intro: Sambucus racemosa f. nuda, also known as the naked elder or American red-berried elder, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its white flowers and red berries. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its berries, roots, and flowers, which are rich in anthocyanins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa f. nuda include its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for colds, flu, and respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of allergies and sinusitis.
